title: Maternal Care Visit Path Stitching
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: maternal care visit path stitching.
assumptions: All resource availability predicates reflect real-time availability and are updated atomically on reservation/release.
Clinical_staff types are pre-qualified for the clinical_services they may be assigned to; qualification checks are out of scope.
Time slots and appointment slots are mutually exclusive for a single staff/resource (no concurrent double-booking allowed).
Patient no-shows and late arrivals are modeled by alternative flows not represented in the core template.
risks: Resource contention: simultaneous reservations may produce conflicts if external systems are not synchronized.
Cascading dependency failures: a missing earlier appointment or resource may block downstream actions, delaying completion.
Incomplete data: missing or incorrect mapping between case and service types can prevent scheduling of required resources.
No-show and cancellation rates could leave idle reserved resources or break assumed sequential dependencies.
